articles.title: Mahmood Faces Backlash Over Rochdale Rapist Sanctions
articles.excerpt: The British Pakistani community expresses discontent over sanctions imposed on a Rochdale rapist, prompting a response from MP Mahmood.
articles.content_html:
A backlash has emerged from the British Pakistani community regarding sanctions imposed on a rapist from Rochdale. Community leaders have voiced concerns that the response from authorities does not reflect the severity of the crime.

Local MP Mahmood faces criticism for the perceived inadequacy of the measures taken. The situation has drawn attention to the actions of authorities in response to the case.

The sanctions have sparked a wider debate about justice and accountability within the community, with calls for more stringent actions against offenders.

As the situation develops, it remains to be seen how the MP and local authorities will address the concerns raised by community members.

articles.title: Police Seek Help to Locate Wanted Man from Rochdale Area
articles.excerpt: Officers are looking for Caylum Smith, who is wanted for failing to appear in court and burglary. He is believed to frequent several areas including Blackley and Salford.
articles.content_html:
Officers are appealing for the public's help to trace a wanted man from the Rochdale area. Caylum Smith, born on 6 August 1997, is wanted for failure to appear at court and burglary.

Smith is believed to frequent the Charleston, Blackley, and Salford areas. Police have urged anyone who may have seen him or has information regarding his whereabouts to come forward.

The appeal comes as part of ongoing efforts to locate individuals wanted by the police in connection with various offences. Authorities are keen to resolve this matter swiftly.

Anyone with information can find Greater Manchester Police and Crimestoppers contact details at the end of this article.

articles.title: BBC Crime Drama 'From Darkness' Filmed in Rochdale Now Available to Stream
articles.excerpt: The BBC crime drama 'From Darkness', filmed in Rochdale, is now available to stream for free, featuring a gripping storyline and a talented cast.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — Updated 20 July 2026 at 07:01. 4 source updates have been combined into this article.

The BBC crime drama 'From Darkness', filmed in Rochdale, is now available to stream for free, featuring a gripping storyline and a talented cast.

Latest update

A BBC crime drama called 'From Darkness', filmed in Rochdale, is currently available to stream for free on the platform U. The series, which first aired in 2015, follows former police officer Claire Church, portrayed by Anne-Marie Duff, as she grapples with an unsolved serial killer case and her own troubled past.

The show features various locations, including Lower Falinge in Rochdale and Manchester. The narrative centres around Claire, who, after leaving Manchester due to the unsettling nature of a case she could not solve, is drawn back to the city and her former partner, DCI John Hind, played by Johnny Harris. The cast also includes notable actors such as Caroline Lee-Johnson, Luke Newberry, and Richard Rankin.

The plot thickens as two victims are discovered at a Manchester building site, linked to the serial killer Claire had previously investigated. The series explores her efforts to piece together clues from both the past and present to confront the killer and her own demons.

In addition to filming in Rochdale and Manchester, some scenes were shot in Argyll, Scotland, which served as a stand-in for the Western Isles. The decision to film on the mainland was reportedly due to logistical considerations. Highlands and Islands Conservative MSP Jamie McGrigor previously noted that some people were disappointed by the filming location choice but expressed hope that it would boost tourism in the region.

Earlier developments

A crime drama called 'From Darkness', which was filmed in Rochdale, is now streaming for free on U. The series originally aired on the BBC in 2015 and follows former police officer Claire Church, portrayed by Anne-Marie Duff, as she grapples with an unresolved serial killer case and her own troubled past.

The show features scenes filmed in Lower Falinge, Rochdale, as well as various locations in Manchester and Scotland. The narrative centres around Claire, who returns to Manchester after leaving due to the unsettling nature of a case she could not solve. She is drawn back to confront her past and her former partner, DCI John Hind, played by Johnny Harris.

In addition to Duff and Harris, the cast includes notable actors such as Caroline Lee-Johnson, Luke Newberry, Leanne Best, Richard Rankin, Emma Edmonson, Lyndsey Marshal, Adrian Rawlins, and Josh Taylor. The plot thickens as Claire investigates two victims found at a Manchester building site, linking them to the serial killer she once pursued.

'From Darkness' has received positive reviews on social media and IMDb, with viewers praising its engaging storyline and performances. The series consists of four one-hour episodes, making it accessible for binge-watching. It is currently available for streaming on U.

Update timeline

  • 20 July 2026, 07:01: BBC Crime Drama 'From Darkness' Filmed in Rochdale Now Available to Stream
  • 20 July 2026, 07:01: BBC Crime Drama 'From Darkness' Filmed in Rochdale Now Streaming for Free

articles.title: Five men arrested in Rochdale during counter-terrorism raid
articles.excerpt: Counter-terrorism police arrested five men in Rochdale, four of whom are Iranian nationals, in connection with a suspected terrorist plot.
articles.content_html:
Counter-terrorism police arrested five men in Rochdale on Sunday in connection with a suspected terrorist plot. The operation involved members of the 22 Special Air Service and Counter Terrorist Specialist Firearms Officers, who detained the men during a raid.

Four of the arrested men are Iranian nationals. The police have not disclosed further details regarding the nature of the suspected plot or the identities of those arrested.

The arrests took place at a residential property in the Rochdale area, where officers were seen detaining a suspect. The police operation was part of an ongoing investigation into potential terrorist activities.

Authorities have not yet provided information on any charges or court appearances related to the arrests. The investigation is ongoing, and further updates are expected in due course.

articles.title: Community Payback Workers Maintain Whitworth Cemetery Grounds
articles.excerpt: A team of community payback workers has been seen mowing and strimming around graves at Whitworth Road Cemetery, demonstrating respect for the site while maintaining its appearance.
articles.content_html:
A team of community payback workers has been observed mowing and strimming around graves at Whitworth Road Cemetery. The workers were noted for their respectful approach to the headstones as they carried out their tasks.

The maintenance work was witnessed by a visitor who had come to clean and strim two graves. The visitor expressed appreciation for the efforts of the community payback team, highlighting their dedication to the upkeep of the cemetery.

Community payback schemes are designed to provide offenders with the opportunity to give back to the community through unpaid work. This initiative not only helps maintain local spaces but also fosters a sense of responsibility among participants.

Whitworth Road Cemetery serves as a significant site for the local community, and the ongoing maintenance efforts contribute to its upkeep and respect for those interred there.

articles.title: Man found guilty of murdering teenager over stolen e-bike in Middleton
articles.excerpt: Terrence King, 60, has been found guilty of murdering 15-year-old Devon Simmonds-Caines, who stole an e-bike from another boy in Middleton last year.
articles.content_html:
A man has been found guilty of murdering a teenager who stole an e-bike. Terrence King, 60, killed 15-year-old Devon Simmonds-Caines by deliberately driving his car into him in Middleton, Greater Manchester, on 22 July 2025.

The jury heard that Simmonds-Caines had stolen the £3,000 bike from another boy on the Langley Estate. Following a fracas, he rode off on the e-bike, prompting King to pursue him in his vehicle.

King admitted to manslaughter but denied murder. However, after a two-week trial, he was convicted of murder and is scheduled to be sentenced on 23 July at Minshull Street Crown Court in Manchester.

The courtroom erupted with screams of joy from the victim's family and friends as the verdict was announced, with shouts of "thank you" heard from those present. CCTV footage captured the moments leading up to the incident, showing the events that unfolded before Simmonds-Caines was struck.

articles.title: Police report made after £380 allegedly stolen from locker at Rochdale Leisure Centre
articles.excerpt: A Rochdale mother says £380 was taken from her purse after a locker key went missing while she was swimming with her children at Rochdale Leisure Centre.
articles.summary: The alleged theft has been reported to police after a visitor said her locker was searched and £380 removed. Leisure-centre staff are also said to have reviewed CCTV footage showing two people of potential interest.
articles.content_html:
A police report has been made after a visitor said £380 was stolen from her locker while she was swimming with her children at Rochdale Leisure Centre.

Sarah-Jane Johnson said she had removed the locker key from her wrist while playing with one of her children and left it beside the pool for no more than 10 minutes.

When the family later left the pool, she said the key could not be found and the contents of the locker appeared to have been searched.

Cash allegedly taken from purse

Johnson said £380 had been removed from her purse. She had reportedly intended to pay the money into a Post Office account the following day.

She described feeling distressed and violated following the incident.

“You just don’t expect something like this to happen when you’re at the swimming baths having a good time with your children.”

CCTV footage reviewed

According to Johnson’s public account, leisure-centre staff reviewed CCTV after the alleged theft and found footage showing two people who may be able to assist with enquiries.

She said images of their faces were available and that the incident had been reported to Greater Manchester Police.

Rochdale Daily has not independently viewed the footage or received confirmation of any identification, arrest or charge.

Warning to other visitors

Johnson urged people using Rochdale Leisure Centre to keep their locker keys with them throughout their visit.

Visitors should also avoid leaving cash or other valuable belongings unattended and should notify staff immediately if a key or access token goes missing.

Report What You Know

Information about this incident: Anyone who witnessed suspicious activity or has relevant information should report it through the Greater Manchester Police online reporting service or call 101.

Anonymous information: Contact Crimestoppers anonymously on 0800 555 111 or submit information anonymously online.

Emergency: Call 999 if a theft is in progress, a suspect remains at the scene or someone is in immediate danger.

This is an ongoing report based on a public account published by Sarah-Jane Johnson. The allegations have not yet been independently confirmed by Greater Manchester Police or Rochdale Leisure Centre. Further verified information will be added when available.
articles.source_name: Greater Manchester Police online reporting service
source_names: Greater Manchester Police online reporting service
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article reports an allegation made in a public social-media post. The circumstances have not been independently established, and no person has been arrested, charged or convicted in connection with the alleged theft. No criminal wrongdoing should be inferred against any identifiable individual unless confirmed by police or a court.

articles.title: UK Opposition Calls for Action Against Pakistan Over Child Sex Offender
articles.excerpt: A former accountability minister has stated that the UK should not exchange dissidents for a convicted rapist in a case linked to Rochdale.
articles.content_html:
A former accountability minister to Imran Khan has expressed strong opposition to the potential exchange of dissidents for Shabbir Ahmed, a convicted rapist, in connection with a case in Rochdale. In an exclusive statement, Shazad Akbar stated that under no circumstances should the UK engage in such an exchange.

Akbar highlighted concerns regarding Pakistan's treatment of dissidents, describing the country as a state accused of persecuting individuals who oppose the government. His comments come amid discussions about the implications of the case for UK-Pakistan relations.

The case has drawn attention due to its sensitive nature and the serious allegations involved. The potential exchange raises questions about the UK's stance on human rights and its responsibilities towards individuals facing persecution.

Anyone with information can find Greater Manchester Police and Crimestoppers contact details at the end of this article.

articles.title: Backlash from British Pakistanis over Rochdale Grooming Gang Deportation Threats
articles.excerpt: Shabana Mahmood plans to amend immigration laws to enable the deportation of Shabir Ahmed, the ringleader of the Rochdale grooming gang, following his recent release from prison.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — Updated 16 July 2026 at 15:59. 22 source updates have been combined into this article.

Shabana Mahmood plans to amend immigration laws to enable the deportation of Shabir Ahmed, the ringleader of the Rochdale grooming gang, following his recent release from prison.

Latest update

Pakistan has blamed the UK for allowing Shabir Ahmed, the leader of the Rochdale grooming gang, to commit numerous sexual offences against girls. Tahir Andrabi, a spokesman for Pakistan's foreign office, stated that the causes of Ahmed's criminality lay in the UK, where he was raised and 'unfortunately spoiled'.

Ahmed, 73, was released from prison on licence two weeks ago after serving 14 years of a 22-year sentence for 30 child rape offences. The UK government is seeking to deport him back to Pakistan, threatening visa restrictions if the country does not accept him. However, Andrabi insisted that Pakistan has 'no connection whatsoever with this matter' and stated that Ahmed must be dealt with under UK laws.

Rochdale councillors have unanimously called for Ahmed's deportation. At a recent town hall meeting, council leader Neil Emmott highlighted the need to close legal loopholes that currently prevent the removal of certain Commonwealth citizens who arrived in the UK over 50 years ago. He expressed support for the victims of the grooming gang, stating that media coverage reopens old wounds for survivors.

The government is considering changes to the law to facilitate Ahmed's deportation, but Pakistan continues to resist any move to return him. Ahmed has been stripped of his British citizenship and is currently without a permanent address, having been moved from Accrington after his location was disclosed on social media.

Earlier developments

Shabana Mahmood plans to amend immigration laws to enable the deportation of Shabir Ahmed, the ringleader of the Rochdale grooming gang, following his recent release from prison.

Ahmed, 73, was released from prison on licence two weeks ago after serving 14 years of a 22-year sentence for 30 child rape offences. The UK government is seeking to deport him back to Pakistan, and Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood has proposed new laws to facilitate this process. However, Pakistan has resisted the move, with Andrabi insisting that Ahmed must be dealt with under UK laws and that Pakistan has no connection to the matter.

Rochdale councillors have unanimously called for Ahmed's deportation, urging the government to close a legal loophole in the Immigration Act 1971 that currently prevents the removal of certain Commonwealth citizens who arrived in the UK over 50 years ago. Ahmed has been stripped of his British citizenship and is left with only Pakistani nationality.

Councillor Neil Emmott, who tabled the motion, expressed support for the victims of the grooming gang, stating that media coverage of the case reopens wounds for survivors. Other councillors echoed the sentiment that Ahmed should not remain in the UK, with Coun Shah Wazir describing him as 'an evil man'.

Shabana Mahmood, the Home Secretary, has announced plans to amend the Immigration Act 1971 to allow for the deportation of Shabir Ahmed, the ringleader of the Rochdale grooming gang. Ahmed, 73, was released from prison earlier this month after serving 14 years of a 22-year sentence for 30 child rape offences. Despite being stripped of his UK citizenship, he cannot be returned to Pakistan due to a legal loophole that protects certain Commonwealth citizens from deportation.

The proposed changes will disapply section seven of the 1971 act for individuals convicted of serious crimes, including child grooming and human trafficking. This amendment aims to ensure that those whose offences are deemed to be of 'exceptional severity' cannot use this loophole to avoid deportation. Mahmood assured Members of Parliament that the changes would not compromise the protections afforded to other Commonwealth citizens, including the Windrush generation.

The move follows increasing pressure from both Labour and Conservative MPs, as well as victims, who have called for Ahmed's deportation. The legal change is expected to be included in Mahmood's immigration and asylum bill, which will be presented to Parliament soon.

In a related development, Ahmed was moved from a bail hostel after his location was disclosed on social media, raising concerns about public safety. The Ministry of Justice confirmed the transfer to an undisclosed location after the details of his address were shared online. Local MPs have expressed their concerns about Ahmed remaining in the North of England, citing him as a continued risk to society.

Update timeline

  • 16 July 2026, 15:59: Pakistan blames Britain for Rochdale rapist’s crimes
  • 16 July 2026, 15:59: Backlash from British Pakistanis over Rochdale Grooming Gang Deportation Threats

articles.title: Fraudster Arrested After Stealing £300,000 from Pensioner
articles.excerpt: A man has been arrested in Tenerife after allegedly stealing £300,000 from a pensioner and spending it on luxury items, including Botox and fine dining.
articles.content_html:
A man has been arrested in Tenerife after allegedly stealing £300,000 from a pensioner in Rochdale. The suspect reportedly used the stolen funds to pay for Botox treatments and lavish meals.

The fraud is said to have taken place over a period of time, during which the pensioner was defrauded of the significant sum. Police are investigating the circumstances surrounding the theft.

Authorities in Tenerife apprehended the man following a tip-off. He is expected to face extradition proceedings to return to the UK to face charges.

Anyone with information can find Greater Manchester Police and Crimestoppers contact details at the end of this article.
